full_suite.failure_notes: Completed in 4,255.08s with 8 warnings. Thirty failures reproduced with both owned files restored to HEAD: 18 pre-lane IMG1605/court-fixture drift failures; 8 managed-sandbox HTTP/Unix-socket failures; test_event_head_eval.py::test_public_eval_persists_probability_maxima_and_nonfinite_count; test_scaffold_tool_index.py::test_real_scaffold_tool_index_matches_checked_in_schema; and two test_truthful_capabilities.py storage-policy failures. The remaining failure, test_train_court_model_v2.py::test_train_court_model_v2_cli_cpu_smoke_beats_random_init, timed out at 540s in the wide suite but passed in 98.936s in isolation with the owned files at HEAD. Static dependency analysis found no path from any failed node to the ABC builder/test changes, but that timeout did not empirically reproduce, so all failures cannot honestly be marked pre-existing.

honest_issues: The mandated wide suite is not green: 31 failures remain outside lane ownership.

Only 30/31 failures reproduced with the E0 diff removed. The court-training timeout is lane-independent by dependency analysis but transient, so the strict pre-existing-failure acceptance item is unmet.

Clean archived HEAD is not a valid dirty-workspace comparator because ignored progress/checkpoint inputs are absent; nodes 2–11 passed there and storage tests could not collect. This is why failures_all_preexisting remains false.
No branch or commit was created; work remains on main. The structured report is for orchestrator capture at runs/lanes/abc_audiofix_20260721/report.json.
